definition: A bridge crossing spanning a set of islands in lake Adamo connecting Edelith with the Arvegum Plains.
A large isle off the coast of Edelith once used as a Ministry fortress against foreign threats, now an urban sanctuary and stronghold of War's forces.
A town at the mouth of Adamo's Cross destroyed in the Wake of Steel by the Arvegum Beast.
A region to the north of Edelith between the northern River Flumen, the eastern Astora Mountains, and the Southern Lake Adamo — primarily known as Edelith's agricultural heart.
A mountain range to the northeast of Edelith separating the Arvegum Plains and the Coronal Valley.
Gloried Goddess of Fertility. Rules over Life, Nature, and Tempest. Her moment is the Flourishing Zenith — high noon at peak power.
A battle between Ministry forces and War's Flamehearts which saw the deployment of devastating arcane weapons — a turning point for the demons in the Wake of Steel.
A frigid and mountainous nation to the north of Solstitheo ruled by the Krastov Clan.
A halo-like structure that floats above Edelith where it is said the gods and angels reside.
Large angelic constructs wielding shields and flaming swords originating from the Circlet of the Sun and tasked with defending Edelith.
A garrison of Conquest's most lethal demons capable of razing entire cities.
A large metallic archer demon among the higher ranks of Conquest's Construct Guard, primarily used to besiege distant lands.
A bountiful valley of deltas to the northeast of Edelith devastated by the demonic wars during the Wake of Steel.
A plague introduced by the demons taking the form of a dark and grimy substance, seeking to deteriorate and consume metal and organic life.
A post-Wake traveller and chronicler. His journals form the backbone of the Travels of a Post-Wake Edelith series.
The densely populated and culturally diverse eastern wing of Edelith.
The north-western peninsula of Edelith characterized by overseas trade, industrial districts, and gothic architecture.
A floral-like synthetic capsule launched from the Circlet of the Sun which holds the power to regrow the wastes with enormous surges of divine power.
Floral-like synthetic capsules launched from the Circlet of the Sun which hold the power to regrow the wastes with enormous surges of divine power.
A lotus-like flower from foreign lands gifted to the people of New Arvegum by the mythic hero Kojin, which when burned emits a calming and demon-repellant smoke.
The elite armies of War who fought with steel weapons capable of igniting flames.
A fort along Adamo's Cross serving as a strategic chokepoint and toll collection center.
Polygonal apparitions comprised of light that serve as the sentinels of the Stillight District.
A conflict between factions serving Edelith's pantheon leading to destabilization within the nation.
A conflict between factions serving Edelith's pantheon leading to destabilization within the nation.
A reservoir to the east of Edelith fed by the rivers of the Coronal Valley to the south.
Shrouded Goddess of Deceit. Rules over Shadow, Trickery, and Twilight. She is the Decaying Dusk — the light that lies about how much remains.
A rebellious faction of the Krastov Clan in Bjornguard who believe the physically strongest should hold power.
The lingering residue of powerful creatures connected to the Circlet of the Sun's immortality.
A Post-Wake settlement within the Cathedral Square of the Eastern Fringe, characterized as the crossroads in Edelith.
An aristocratic estate that became the site of a notable Inquisition. Details remain classified by the Steelside Legion.
The governing and clerical body of Solstitheo, residing in the Upper Raise of the capitol, Edelith.
A Post-Wake settlement formed at the foot of the Astora Mountains known for its festivity-oriented culture.
A region of the wastes east of Edelith defined by its ebony sands and towering crystalline monoliths.
Enlightened God of Innovation. Rules over Forge, Knowledge, and War. His domain is the Rising Dawn — the moment of first full light.
An effect of divine light that purges disease and severs one's immortality from the Circlet of the Sun.
A lineage of warriors who carry a serpentine affliction in their bloodline. Considered cursed by some, chosen by others.
The conic barrier of light cast by the Circlet of the Sun that encompasses all of Edelith, granting immortality to select creatures within.
A term popularized in Post-Wake Edelith referring to those who ventured into Edelith through openings in the Shroud of Aurum, often regarded as inferior to those Edelith-born.
An educational institute in the Stillight District focused on technological and arcane pursuits.
Realized God of Destiny. Rules over Ambition, Arcana, and Life. Associated with the Igniting Daybreak — the first light before sunrise.
The nation and world surrounding the Immortal City of Edelith, home to the five-deity Solstitheian pantheon.
A battalion of metal-wielding soldiers in Post-Wake Edelith promoting the repulsion of demons and regional stability.
Originally known as the Upper Raise, and additionally as the Stillight Quarter, the fortified, federal, and central district of Edelith inaccessible due to arcane lanterns shunning intruders.
A repurposed quarry in the wastes covered by a large tent used as a settlement.
A region to the north of Edelith burnt down by War's Flamehearts.
The drained bay between the northern Arvegum Plains, the eastern Lake Adamo, the western Ardeat Citadel, and the southern city of Edelith — once the site of a metropolis, now abandoned.
An arcane current of electricity stored within canisters utilized by the Steelside Legion to enhance their metal attributes' performance and resistance against the Corrosion.
Silenced Deity of Tranquility. Rules over Grave, Order, and Peace. Represents the Smoldering Nightfall — the last warmth before dark.
An event in Solstitheian history where swarms of metallic demons emerged from fissures in the earth, seeking to consume everything with their Corrosion.
The economic and noble western wing of Edelith.
A Ministry strike force led by the original General Levant Strom who perished in the Crystallic Skirmish.
A militia of incorporated Tarpaul Pit gang members serving the imposter General Strom with the purpose of retrieving Edelith Pods from the desert.
A race of wooden constructs created by and adhering to the god Hiebrief.
H.D. Yordenriser — a historian whose chronicle attempts to objectively record Edelith's timeline. Reliability disputed.
